title: MSK Embryology: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of MSK Embryology
Identify key diagnostic features of MSK Embryology
Apply clinical reasoning to MSK Embryology management
Recognize common pitfalls in MSK Embryology

sections.title: Overview of MSK Embryology
sections.content:
**MSK Embryology**

Somite differentiation (sclerotome, myotome, dermatome), limb development, bone formation (endochondral vs intramembranous)

**Key Points:**
• Somites → sclerotome (vertebrae/ribs), myotome (skeletal muscle), dermatome (dermis)
• Limb buds: AER (apical ectodermal ridge) → proximal-distal growth
• ZPA (zone of polarizing activity) → anterior-posterior patterning (SHH gene)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Somites → sclerotome (vertebrae/ribs), myotome (skeletal muscle), dermatome (dermis)
• Limb buds: AER (apical ectodermal ridge) → proximal-distal growth

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• ZPA (zone of polarizing activity) → anterior-posterior patterning (SHH gene)
• Endochondral ossification: cartilage template → bone (long bones)
• Intramembranous ossification: mesenchyme → bone directly (flat bones of skull)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for MSK Embryology:**

• ZPA (zone of polarizing activity) → anterior-posterior patterning (SHH gene)
• Endochondral ossification: cartilage template → bone (long bones)
• Intramembranous ossification: mesenchyme → bone directly (flat bones of skull)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of MSK Embryology?

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for MSK Embryology?

options.text: Intramembranous ossification: mesenchyme → bone directly (flat bones of skull)
options.explanation: Intramembranous ossification: mesenchyme → bone directly (flat bones of skull)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with MSK Embryology. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Limb buds: AER (apical ectodermal ridge) → proximal-distal growth
options.explanation: Limb buds: AER (apical ectodermal ridge) → proximal-distal growth
